summary: This record previously tracked a privacy-category duplicate that wrote HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ReserveManager\DisableDeletes = 1 directly. It was retired on 2026-03-13 because the repo already contains the proper official command-backed tweak cleanup.disable-reserved-storage, which uses the documented DISM Reserved Storage surface. This record remains only as an audit trail from the retired privacy ID to the surviving cleanup tweak.

setting.name: Disable Reserved Storage (Deprecated Privacy Duplicate)
setting.casual_explanation: This privacy-category record is deprecated. Use cleanup.disable-reserved-storage for the supported Reserved Storage control.
setting.why_people_change_it: This record is no longer used for research or implementation decisions because the duplicate privacy tweak was retired in favor of the official cleanup tweak.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Keeping the deprecated record preserves the old ID and explains why the duplicate was removed without leaving future research to reason about the wrong registry-only surface.
setting.professional_notes: The retired privacy tweak wrote ReserveManager\DisableDeletes directly. Research confirmed that Reserved Storage is officially documented through DISM command and API surfaces, and the repo already had a command-backed cleanup.disable-reserved-storage tweak implementing that official model. The privacy duplicate was therefore removed from PrivacyTweakProvider.cs instead of being fixed in place.

Prevents future research from treating the old registry-only surface as a current source of truth.

evidence.title: Reserved Storage duplicate retirement audit trail
evidence.summary: The privacy.disable-reserved-storage duplicate was retired on 2026-03-13 because the repo already exposes the official DISM-backed cleanup.disable-reserved-storage tweak. The privacy duplicate had written ReserveManager\DisableDeletes directly, while the surviving cleanup tweak uses the documented DISM detect/apply/rollback surface.

decision.why: Deprecated after duplicate retirement. The privacy ID pointed to an internal registry side effect while the repo already had a proper official cleanup tweak for Reserved Storage. This record now exists only as an audit trail.

The privacy duplicate had written ReserveManager\DisableDeletes directly, while the surviving cleanup tweak uses the documented DISM detect/apply/rollback surface.
